disclaimer: Operational compliance research and cited public-records compilation — not legal advice. Confirm motor-carrier, TNC, dealer, mover, and repair requirements with the relevant state authority.
policy: Public .gov sources only; 403/404 = park (no bypass); gaps explicit.

regulator.agency: PA Public Utility Commission — Motor Carrier
regulator.notes: Intrastate property/passenger/HHG carriers; insurance Forms E/H

cited_facts.text: The Pennsylvania Public Utility Commission regulates nearly 7,500 motor carriers that transport property, passengers, or household goods for compensation in Pennsylvania.

citation.source: PA PUC — Motor Carrier

cited_facts.text: Property carriers (e.g., trucking companies) operating in Pennsylvania are regulated by the PUC for safety and insurance requirements only (not rates).

citation.source: PA PUC — Motor Carrier

cited_facts.text: Motor carriers must file binding evidence of bodily-injury/property-damage liability (Form E) and cargo insurance (Form H) with the PUC via Tyler Insurance Filings; brokerage authority requires surety bond evidence.

citation.source: PA PUC — Motor Carrier Insurance

cited_facts.text: The Unified Carrier Registration (UCR) Act requires interstate motor carriers, freight forwarders, brokers, and leasing companies to register and pay an annual fee.

citation.source: PA PUC — UCR

regulator.notes: TNC license + insurance; Title 66 Ch. 26 (Act 164 of 2016)

cited_facts.text: Transportation Network Companies (TNCs) such as Uber, Lyft, and Z Trip must hold a PUC-issued license to operate and maintain insurance on file with the PUC Insurance Section.

citation.source: PA PUC — Limos, Taxis, TNCs & Movers

cited_facts.text: Pennsylvania TNC rules are in Title 66 (Public Utilities) Chapter 26 — Transportation Network Service; Chapter 26 was added November 4, 2016 (Act 164 of 2016), effective immediately.

citation.source: PA Consolidated Statutes Title 66 Ch. 26

regulator.notes: Certificated HHG carriers; written estimates; 60¢/lb default coverage

cited_facts.text: Household goods movers operating within Pennsylvania must be licensed by the PUC, maintain adequate insurance, and charge PUC-approved fees.

citation.source: PA PUC — Limos, Taxis, TNCs & Movers

cited_facts.text: Before hiring a mover, consumers must receive a written estimate; if distance is ≤40 miles estimates may be hourly per tariff, if >40 miles weight-and-mileage basis applies.

citation.source: PA PUC — Limos, Taxis, TNCs & Movers

cited_facts.text: Default loss/damage protection for household goods moves is 60 cents per pound per article unless additional coverage is purchased.

citation.source: PA PUC — Limos, Taxis, TNCs & Movers

regulator.notes: 75 Pa.C.S. Ch. 13; MV-375 bond; MV-349 dealer plates; 67 Pa. Code Ch. 43/53

cited_facts.text: Pennsylvania vehicle dealer licensing and related requirements are governed by Title 75 (Vehicles) Chapter 13 — Dealer Plates and Dealer Licensing.

citation.source: PA Consolidated Statutes Title 75 Ch. 13

cited_facts.text: Manufacturer/Dealer surety bond: $20,000 for each place of business (Form MV-375); bond must be maintained continuously or dealer/agent authorization is void.

citation.source: PennDOT Form MV-375 (3-25)

cited_facts.text: Dealer onboarding requires Form MV-349 (dealer registration plates), MV-375 original bond per place of business, and a vehicle dealership lot license from PA Department of State.

citation.source: PennDOT PUB 459 — Dealer Requirements

cited_facts.text: Temporary registration plate inventory: dealers must report plate inventory within 30 days of Bureau request; plates stored in Bureau-approved secure location (67 Pa. Code § 43.7).

citation.source: 67 Pa. Code § 43.7

regulator.agency: FTC Consumer Advice (federal) + PA consumer protection
regulator.notes: No PA statewide automotive repair shop license analogous to CA BAR

cited_facts.text: The best way to avoid auto repair rip-offs is to be prepared — know how your vehicle works, how to choose a good mechanic, and your consumer rights.

citation.source: FTC Consumer Advice — Auto Repair Basics

cited_facts.text: Pennsylvania does not operate a statewide automotive repair shop license analogous to California BAR — shop compliance is primarily contract/consumer-protection driven unless performing emissions/safety inspection under separate PennDOT programs.

citation.source: FTC Consumer Advice — Auto Repair Basics

federal_context.text: The Pennsylvania Public Utility Commission regulates nearly 7,500 motor carriers that transport property, passengers, or household goods for compensation in Pennsylvania.
Property carriers (e.g., trucking companies) operating in Pennsylvania are regulated by the PUC for safety and insurance requirements only (not rates).

cited_facts.text: Registration Alerts The Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration (FMCSA) is notifying the public that a HIGH PHISHING ALERT regarding FAKE SAFETY AUDITS are being sent by email to registered entities by someone pretending to be FMCSA.

citation.source: PA Public Utility Commission — Motor Carrier

cited_facts.text: Property carriers (e.g., trucking companies) and charter bus carriers (seating capacity of more than 15) are regulated for safety and insurance requirements only.

citation.source: PA Public Utility Commission — Motor Carrier

cited_facts.text: Property carriers (e.g., trucking companies) and charter bus carriers (seating capacity of more than 15) are regulated for safety and insurance requirements only.

citation.source: PA Public Utility Commission — Motor Carrier

cited_facts.text: Property carriers (e.g., trucking companies) and charter bus carriers (seating capacity of more than 15) are regulated for safety and insurance requirements only.

citation.source: PA Public Utility Commission — Motor Carrier

cited_facts.text: Registration Alerts The Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration (FMCSA) is notifying the public that a HIGH PHISHING ALERT regarding FAKE SAFETY AUDITS are being sent by email to registered entities by someone pretending to be FMCSA.

citation.source: PA Public Utility Commission — Motor Carrier
